Title: Pax Bharata
Author: Demeisen
Description:
A new Scenario that replaces the politics of 2026 Earth with 170 novel nations, with their own alliances, claims, federations, and unification/fragmentation projects.

The divergence is old: the Mongol khanates never fully receded. Five hordes still hold the steppe from the Urals to Manchuria, China never reunified — a dozen rival states squabble where one empire should be — and in Baghdad the Abbasid Caliphate never fell, a millennium-old center of learning aging into a vast, restless nuclear state.

With the land roads taxed by khans, the merchant powers of the Indian subcontinent took to the sea instead. They colonized Africa, Southeast Asia, and Australia, and reached the Americas across the Pacific — but war broke out before their colonial ambitions could reach those shores. Nine great powers rule the waves from Zanzibar to Honolulu; Persian is the language of diplomacy, and no tongue called English ever left its island.

There has been exactly one world war: the War of All Shores (1794–1807), sparked when Vanga tolled the straits to the Pacific and invaded Timor to close the last free channel. It ended with the Concord of Kala — the Great Renunciation — which turned colonial provinces into sovereign allied nations and opened 219 years of Pax Bharata. Europe, never the protagonist, is a fractured periphery of kingdoms, tsardoms, and merchant republics; the Americas answer to no colonizer at all.

Now the peace is cracking. The old regimes look stable, but inequality gnaws at their foundations, border disputes seethe on every frontier, and for the first time in living memory there is war on the subcontinent itself: Punjabi tanks roll towards New Delhi. The world would call it the shock of the century — if an alien craft had not just fallen out of the sky.

No nation dominates this Earth, and none can be dismissed: every country on the map holds two, three, or four control points — no minnows, no whales. The webs of diplomacy and war offer dozens of paths to unifying most of the world. Uniting all the world's people under one human flag? There is exactly one way.

Pick the 'Pax Bharata' scenario when starting a new campaign. Vanilla scenarios remain playable with the mod enabled.

Known Limitation: unification and fragmentation projects are scenario-locked but not scenario-hidden — in Pax Bharata the standard 2026 projects are visible in the tech tree but can never be unlocked, and in vanilla scenarios the Pax Bharata projects likewise appear but can never be unlocked.

Supported game version: built against the current (pre-Dark Skies) build of Terra Invicta. A compatibility update will follow shortly after Dark Skies launches.

Full history of the world: https://demeisen6.github.io/pax-bharata/
Bug reports and feedback: https://github.com/demeisen6/pax-bharata/issues
